article_title: The effects of sleep deprivation and extreme exertion on cognitive performance at the world-record breaking Suffolk Back Yard Ultra-marathon
raw_text: 2CRT Reaction Time: Δ 77±68 ms; p = 0.004, Cohen's d = 1.09 [0.33–1.81]
textual_interpretation: Reaction time increased (worsened) post-race in the 2CRT task
notes: Table 1, paired t-test, N=11. t-value calculated from Δ=77, SD=68, n=11: t=77/(68/√11)=3.76

article_title: The effects of sleep deprivation and extreme exertion on cognitive performance at the world-record breaking Suffolk Back Yard Ultra-marathon
textual_interpretation: Throughput in the 2CRT task decreased (worsened) post-race
notes: Table 1, paired t-test, N=11. t-value calculated from Δ=-17.0, SD=11.4: t=-17.0/(11.4/√11)=-4.94

article_title: The effects of sleep deprivation and extreme exertion on cognitive performance at the world-record breaking Suffolk Back Yard Ultra-marathon
textual_interpretation: No significant change in 2CRT accuracy post-race
notes: Table 1, Wilcoxon signed-rank test (non-parametric, data marked 'a'), N=11. Z-value not reported

article_title: The effects of sleep deprivation and extreme exertion on cognitive performance at the world-record breaking Suffolk Back Yard Ultra-marathon
raw_text: Stroop Level 3 accuracy: Δ -2.5±2.8%; p = 0.014, Cohen's d = 0.52 [-0.12–1.15]
textual_interpretation: Level 3 accuracy in the Stroop task decreased (worsened) post-race
notes: Table 1, paired t-test, N=11. t-value calculated from Δ=-2.5, SD=2.8: t=-2.5/(2.8/√11)=-2.96

article_title: The effects of sleep deprivation and extreme exertion on cognitive performance at the world-record breaking Suffolk Back Yard Ultra-marathon
raw_text: Stroop Level 3 Reaction Time: Δ 91±119 ms; p = 0.029, Cohen's d = 0.77 [0.79–1.44]
textual_interpretation: Reaction time in the Stroop task Level 3 increased (worsened) post-race
notes: Table 1, paired t-test, N=11. t-value calculated from Δ=91, SD=119: t=91/(119/√11)=2.54

article_title: The effects of sleep deprivation and extreme exertion on cognitive performance at the world-record breaking Suffolk Back Yard Ultra-marathon
raw_text: Stroop Interference Score: Δ -4.3±5.6 a.u.; p = 0.028, Cohen's d = 0.53 [-0.12–1.15]
textual_interpretation: Executive function was impaired: Interference Score decreased (worsened) post-race
notes: Table 1, paired t-test, N=11. t-value calculated from Δ=-4.3, SD=5.6: t=-4.3/(5.6/√11)=-2.55

article_title: The effects of sleep deprivation and extreme exertion on cognitive performance at the world-record breaking Suffolk Back Yard Ultra-marathon
raw_text: Tower Puzzle Mean Reaction Time: Δ 517±296 ms; p = 0.003, Cohen's d = 1.75 [0.77–2.69]
textual_interpretation: Reaction time in Tower Puzzle increased (worsened) post-race
notes: Table 1, Wilcoxon signed-rank test (non-parametric, data marked 'a'), N=11. Z-value not reported

article_title: The effects of sleep deprivation and extreme exertion on cognitive performance at the world-record breaking Suffolk Back Yard Ultra-marathon
raw_text: Tower Puzzle Mean Score: Δ -105.1±303.5 a.u.; p = 0.248, Cohen's d = 0.25 [-0.35–0.85]
textual_interpretation: No significant change in Tower Puzzle Mean Score (decision making not affected)
notes: Table 1, Wilcoxon signed-rank test (non-parametric, data marked 'a'), N=11. Z-value not reported

article_title: The effects of sleep deprivation and extreme exertion on cognitive performance at the world-record breaking Suffolk Back Yard Ultra-marathon
raw_text: Tower Puzzle Move Ratio: Δ 0.07±0.17 a.u.; p = 0.240, Cohen's d = 0.42 [-0.20–1.03]
textual_interpretation: No significant change in Tower Puzzle Move Ratio
notes: Table 1, paired t-test, N=11. t-value calculated from Δ=0.07, SD=0.17: t=0.07/(0.17/√11)=1.37

article_title: The effects of sleep deprivation and extreme exertion on cognitive performance at the world-record breaking Suffolk Back Yard Ultra-marathon
raw_text: Total average time asleep 7-day vs night prior: 7.29±0.96 vs 6.78±1.32 hours, p = 0.042, d = 0.44
textual_interpretation: Total average time asleep in the 7 days before the race was higher compared to the night prior to the race
notes: Paired t-test, N=11. t-value not reported explicitly

article_title: The effects of sleep deprivation and extreme exertion on cognitive performance at the world-record breaking Suffolk Back Yard Ultra-marathon
raw_text: Time in bed: 7-day 8.31±1.30 vs night prior 8.26±0.99, p = 0.803
textual_interpretation: No statistical difference in time in bed between 7-day average and night prior
notes: Paired t-test, N=11. t-value not reported

article_title: The effects of sleep deprivation and extreme exertion on cognitive performance at the world-record breaking Suffolk Back Yard Ultra-marathon
raw_text: Sleep quality: 7-day 3.68±0.58 vs night prior 3.45±0.93, p = 0.355
textual_interpretation: No statistical difference in sleep quality between 7-day average and night prior
notes: Paired t-test, N=11. t-value not reported

article_title: The effects of sleep deprivation and extreme exertion on cognitive performance at the world-record breaking Suffolk Back Yard Ultra-marathon
raw_text: Average sleep ratings 7-day vs 2CRT ΔRT: r = -0.64, p = 0.034
textual_interpretation: A moderate negative association was observed between the average sleep ratings over 7 days prior to the race and the 2CRT ΔRT — higher sleep quality associated with smaller increase in RT
notes: Table 3 and Fig 2a, Spearman correlation, N=11

article_title: The effects of sleep deprivation and extreme exertion on cognitive performance at the world-record breaking Suffolk Back Yard Ultra-marathon
raw_text: Average sleep ratings 7-day vs 2CRT ΔTP: r = 0.61, p = 0.045
textual_interpretation: There was a moderate association between the average sleep ratings over 7 days and the 2CRT ΔTP — higher sleep quality associated with smaller decline in throughput
notes: Table 3 and Fig 2b, Spearman correlation, N=11

article_title: The effects of sleep deprivation and extreme exertion on cognitive performance at the world-record breaking Suffolk Back Yard Ultra-marathon
raw_text: Total average time asleep vs average sleep rating 7-day: r = 0.70, p = 0.016
textual_interpretation: There was a strong correlation between total average time asleep and average sleep rating in the 7 days prior to the race
notes: Table 3, correlation type inferred as Spearman (sleep rating marked *), N=11

article_title: The effects of sleep deprivation and extreme exertion on cognitive performance at the world-record breaking Suffolk Back Yard Ultra-marathon
raw_text: Time asleep night prior vs sleep rating 7-day: r = 0.62, p = 0.041
textual_interpretation: Moderate correlation between time asleep night prior and 7-day average sleep rating

article_title: The effects of sleep deprivation and extreme exertion on cognitive performance at the world-record breaking Suffolk Back Yard Ultra-marathon
raw_text: Sleep rating night prior vs sleep rating 7-day: r = 0.68, p = 0.022
textual_interpretation: Moderate-to-strong correlation between sleep rating night prior and 7-day average sleep rating

article_title: The effects of sleep deprivation and extreme exertion on cognitive performance at the world-record breaking Suffolk Back Yard Ultra-marathon
raw_text: Average sleep rating 7-day vs Δ Interference Score: r = 0.24, p = 0.473
textual_interpretation: No significant correlation between 7-day sleep rating and change in Stroop Interference Score

article_title: The effects of sleep deprivation and extreme exertion on cognitive performance at the world-record breaking Suffolk Back Yard Ultra-marathon
raw_text: Total distance vs Δ2CRT RT: r = 0.25, p = 0.457; Total distance vs Δ2CRT TP: r = -0.10, p = 0.769; Total distance vs Δ Interference: r = -0.26, p = 0.448
textual_interpretation: There was no significant correlation between the total distances covered and cognitive performance
notes: Table 3, representative value for total distance vs Δ2CRT RT (Spearman). All distance-cognition correlations non-significant, N=11
